Call: the proper name that forces a hidden thing into presence
address that reaches its true object and compels an answer
speech that reveals by naming what something really is
invocation, testimony, and recognition made operative
the fitting word that strips illusion and makes truth answerable
a declaration so exact that vagueness can no longer survive
language as witness, summoning what was concealed into clarity
to call a thing rightly and restore it to its proper place
Veil: concealment that protects by refusing full availability
hiddenness that is present, meaningful, and not yet surrendered
the obscurity that preserves depth from premature exposure
silence, secrecy, and inwardness gathered under one shadow
a mask that guards rather than merely deceives
the dream's power to imply without fully disclosing
what remains half-seen because revelation is not always kindness
Media: that through which meaning passes across separation
the channel that carries relation without erasing difference
translation, transmission, and contact made possible by a living middle
the bridge of sign, rite, water, or symbol between divided things
what makes a boundary traversable without denying the boundary
the conduit by which thought survives the journey between minds and worlds
all means by which the distant becomes communicable
the force of connection itself, neither source nor destination but the way between
Figure: shape held clearly enough to remain itself
coherence, boundary, and pattern preserved against confusion
the stable form by which identity becomes legible and endures
containment that allows a thing to exist as a distinct presence
the structure that keeps a role, body, or thought from coming apart
law, outline, and proportion gathered into durable relation
what binds scattered parts into one recognizable whole
the integrity by which a thing persists instead of dissolving
Drift: meaning that slides into another shape without fully breaking
the slippage by which a word, self, or role becomes otherwise
semantic drift, dream metamorphosis, and unstable identity
change by displacement, reinterpretation, and altered relation
the current that carries forms into new uses and stranger recognitions
the dream's habit of letting one thing become another by degrees
the loosening of categories, names, and fixed meanings
transformation hidden inside every stable form
Echo: what returns after the first saying and continues to act
repetition as resonance, memory, and altered recurrence
the lingering force of words, rites, and wounds that keep sounding
a reflected voice that becomes refrain, tradition, or haunting
memory made active as response and return
what survives by being called back again
the after-sound of a truth or promise that does not end cleanly
resonance that binds separated moments into one pattern
Fade: meaning thinning out until it can no longer bear its old weight
the quiet erosion by which names, forms, and memories lose their hold
forgetting as gradual dimming into distance
a force weakening until only a pale trace remains
the slow recession of clarity, structure, and significance
what is not destroyed but allowed to slip beyond effective grasp
language worn smooth until its old force can no longer be felt
the long diminishment by which things pass from presence into near-loss
undeniably gone, never to be seen again
